ideas.physics_build_review: The core physics claim is partially real: 5.0% in-band EUV conversion efficiency from a 2-micron-driven preshaped tin microdroplet is peer-reviewed (ARCNL, Appl. Phys. Lett. 123, 234101 (2023) + erratum, P3R2-D-11-S03), exceeding the judge's CE>=4% number -- but at 10 Hz, three to four orders of magnitude below the 50,000-100,000 droplets/s an industrial source runs (P3R2-D-11-S01), on a thermally unloaded target with no debris/replenishment loading. Scaling the drive chain (Tm:fiber coherent combining feeding Ho:YLF/Ho:YAG) to kW average power faces unproven TMI/photodarkening limits at 2 um (L12-016/017); TRL 2 is honest. The 2026-29 path (100 W ps source, droplet CE campaign, 300 W module) is executable only as an academic/consortium program requiring droplet-target chamber access few labs have; the $600k experiment is 2.4x the rubric budget band. The hidden showstopper is not CE but rep-rate thermal, debris, and collector-lifetime engineering the seed does not own -- and a sole buyer class whose lead technologist publicly states CO2 scales to 1,500-2,000 W without an architecture change.

G1.rationale: No primary buyer/procurement source exists: the seed's own demand IDs are mis-cited (L12-034 is IPG Photonics' 10-K; L12-053 is a Gigaphoton vendor datasheet with demand_evidence_type 'none' -- confirmed in the atlas ledger), and the only real signal is a $12M DOE MSRC research hedge with ASML as partner (P3R2-D-11-S02), which is exploratory R&D, not procurement. ASML's own 2026 roadmap statement (P3R2-D-11-S01) is a negative demand signal.

G2.rationale: The load-bearing CE claim is affirmatively peer-reviewed (APL 123, 234101 (2023), P3R2-D-11-S03); supporting fiber/TMI literature (L12-001/002/016/017) is atlas-accepted.

G3.rationale: A bounded kill/continue experiment exists (2028 droplet CE campaign, CE>=3% gate) but at $600k it is 2.4x the $250k band and requires consortium beam-time/chamber access outside founder control.

G4.rationale: Closest competitors named and real (TRUMPF CO2 incumbent L12-039; LLNL BAT program P3R2-D-11-S02; IPG/Coherent 2-um programs); the wavelength-architecture difference is non-cosmetic.

G5.rationale: The entire market depends on a single contingent event -- ASML abandoning CO2 scaling -- that the incumbent's own 2026 public roadmap contradicts (P3R2-D-11-S01), and the 5% CE operating point is unverified at industrial rep-rate; that is dependence on one unverifiable market forecast plus unproven physics at the claimed operating point.

G6.rationale: Export walls are honestly acknowledged: EUV components are export-restricted from China (P3R2-D-11-S04), China is correctly excluded, and the US/JP base case is legally clean.

G7.rationale: The evidence file's own timing verdict is 'uncertain-to-closed by 2030': the sole official trigger is a research-timeline award (through ~2029), not a 2030-2034 procurement trigger, and the independent second timing source (ASML's 2,000 W CO2 path) argues the window may not open in the mission window at all.

ideas.elimination_reason: G1 fail (plus G5 and G7 fails): no primary buyer or procurement evidence exists for 2-micron drive modules -- the seed's demand citations are mis-attributed vendor/competitor filings; the only real signal is a government research hedge; the sole buyer's own public roadmap (CO2 to 1,500-2,000 W) contradicts the timing thesis; the physics gate (CE>=4% at relevant rep-rate) is unmet by 3-4 orders of magnitude in repetition rate; and China is foreclosed by export controls. A capped research option, not a 2030 company.
ideas.uncertainty_notes: Elimination is robust on current evidence. Revival conditions: an ASML/Gigaphoton procurement signal or LOI for 2-um drive capability, or demonstrated CE>=4% at >=10 kHz with debris data. The atlas itself has a citation-hygiene defect (L12 lane markdown attributes ASML annual-report content to L12-034, which the ledger records as IPG's 10-K) that should be repaired.
redteam_flags_for_p5: If revisited: verify whether any DOE MSRC/LLNL BAT milestone converts to an ASML co-funding or procurement commitment by 2028.
Audit the L12-034 citation collision in the atlas (ASML vs IPG content) before any source is reused.

ideas.physics_build_review: EHD conduction pumping is documented lab physics (L16-001/002) with modest pressure heads; +/-20% per-channel flow modulation at AI-package heat fluxes is plausible, but the decisive claim -- beating an optimized mechanical-pump cold plate on CHF and hotspot-following at equal pumping power -- is untested, and both 2025 reviews the seed honestly cites (L16-003/004) confirm zero industrial deployments. Electrode fouling, EMI, and 1,000 h stability in candidate fluids are open; the hard fluid-intersection gate (simultaneously dielectric, non-PFAS, low-GWP) is a chemistry constraint outside founder control that may be an empty set after EU REACH finalizes. TRL 3 is honest. The 2026-29 path (pump-cell characterization, 500 W emulator, OEM eval units) is executable in a university thermal lab and the $180k first experiment sits inside the budget band with a correct parity-equals-kill rule. Service burden is attractively low (no moving parts) if electrodes last. The physics showstopper to watch: EHD conduction pumping weakens in high-vapor-quality regimes -- exactly where hotspot-following matters most.

G1.rationale: Two independent demand sources exist for the function: Google's Project Deschutes buyer specification (L14-043, primary) plus the dated PFAS/HFC regulatory forcing (P3R2-D-12-S01, L14-033/034) and ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS (L14-030). EHD-specific demand is nil -- no OEM co-development found, and the $19B consolidation wave (P3R2-D-12-S02) signals build/buy-in-house behavior. Flip: the seed's own 2028 named-OEM gate unmet at checkpoint means fail.

G2.rationale: Technical basis rests on peer-reviewed reviews and papers (L16-001..004, L14-006/009/010), which honestly report the deployment gap.

G3.rationale: $180k, 2027, decisive design (must beat best mechanical plate; parity = kill) -- inside the $25k-250k band and genuinely kill-capable.

G4.rationale: Named competitors (Flex/JetCool L14-045, ZutaCore L14-046, Vertiv L14-048, Eaton/Boyd and Ecolab/CoolIT per P3R2-D-12-S02) are all mechanical-pump-based; in-plate field-driven flow control is a non-cosmetic difference.

G5.rationale: No nonexistent physics, but two stacked contingencies: EHD must outperform mechanical pumping industrially (never demonstrated), and a compliant working fluid must exist; both are codified as kill gates, which is the correct honest structure.

G6.rationale: No export-control exposure (low-voltage thermal component); PFAS/F-gas/REACH regulatory path is mapped, and GB 40879's unfinalized status is correctly not overstated.

G7.rationale: Dated triggers exist (AIM Act 30%-of-baseline step 2029-2033 L14-033; EU REACH PFAS opinions ~end-2026; GB 40879 draft), but the OEM consolidation wave could close the outside-licensor window before 2030, and the 2028 demand-bridge gate is unmet as of 2026-07.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: Capacity/regulatory demand is primary and dated (L14-043 buyer spec; L14-033/034; P3R2-D-12-S01 fluid exit), but no buyer has expressed EHD-specific demand and the 2028 OEM bridge is unbuilt.

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: Field-driven, silent, per-square-millimeter boiling control (L16-001/002) against 1,000 W/cm2 hotspot roadmaps (L14-010) is a genuine frontier concept with credible expansion to power electronics and laser diodes.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: Per-channel flow balancing in two-phase plates is unsolved and valuable (L14-043 3C-approach spec), but reachable only through OEMs now consolidating under Eaton/Ecolab (P3R2-D-12-S02).

competition_whitespace.evidence: No competitor offers in-plate field-driven control (L14-045/046/048 all mechanical), but incumbents are massively capitalized post-consolidation and mechanical pumping is adequate today.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: $180k decisive 2027 experiment inside band; v1 capital $4-10M with a licensable component model plausibly near the $5M line at the low end.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Measurable and testable in bounded steps, but the fluid-qualification dependency (dielectric+non-PFAS+low-GWP) sits outside the team's control loop.

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: Milliwatt drive power and zero local moving parts is a qualitative edge (L16-001/002), but the quantitative edge over mechanical pumping is exactly what the 2027 experiment must prove; reviews (L16-003/004) counsel skepticism.

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: US buyer ecosystem strong; CN leg rests on unfinalized GB 40879 (L14-035) with no CN buyer evidence found this session.

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: Problem window open now (fluid crisis dated 2025-2026, P3R2-D-12-S01); risk that consolidated incumbents fill it in-house before a 2030 launch.

expansion_economics.evidence: Same pump cells extend to power electronics, EV charging, laser cooling if the core works.

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: Founder_fit_note: HV drive, flow modulation, and feedback control fit; microchannel two-phase design and fluid qualification are outside the profile.

ideas.uncertainty_notes: Score hinges on an unrun 2027 experiment and an unresolved fluid-chemistry intersection; demand side is well-documented but product-specific demand is zero as of 2026-07. Low end reflects OEM in-house capture; high end reflects a decisive experimental win plus a compliant fluid emerging.
redteam_flags_for_p5: Attack the licensing thesis: post-consolidation (Eaton/Boyd $9.5B, Ecolab/CoolIT $4.75B), why would any OEM license EHD rather than build or acquire?
Verify at least one dielectric+non-PFAS+low-GWP fluid candidate survives EU REACH final opinions with usable dielectric strength.

ideas.physics_build_review: Latent-heat storage plus two-phase spreading is settled physics; the engineering challenge is integration at scale -- 300+ MJ at 100+ W/cm2 source flux implies roughly 1.2+ tonnes of PCM (at ~250 kJ/kg) with engineered conductivity enhancement (graphite matrices) and encapsulation cycle life, all quantifiable. TRL 4 is defensible given General Atomics demonstrated a 3 MJ / 35 kg / 230 kW wax-PCM DEW module in 2010 (P3R2-D-13-S02) -- which also proves the seed's 'unique in pool / no productized LRU' claim wrong. The $220k 100 kW-for-60 s brassboard (2027) is executable in a university/SBIR setting and measures the three governing quantities (flux, plate excursion, cycle fade) directly. The slow parts are MIL-qualification and prime integration, standard but multi-year. Service burden is moderate (sealed LRU with telemetry). Showstopper watch: on mobile platforms the tonne-scale mass trade versus a bigger chiller must close at 300-500 kW, and it fails if fielded lasers plateau at 150 kW.

G1.rationale: Primary government procurement exists: JLWS OTA awards ($86M initial to nLIGHT Defense and Lockheed Aculight under an $847M ceiling, P3R2-D-13-S01, 2026-07-10) plus the independent E-HEL program of record (24 units, FY2027 initial procurement, P3R2-D-13-S06). Note: the seed's GAO SWaP-C citation (L12-045) was mis-characterized and does not carry the demand case -- the OTAs do.

G2.rationale: Technical sources (L14-001/024/025/027) are atlas-accepted; no unreviewed academic claim is load-bearing.

G3.rationale: $220k 2027 brassboard with quantitative pass bars (>=100 W/cm2, <10 K excursion, 500 cycles) -- bounded and near the budget band.

G4.rationale: The seed's 'no productized burst-mode thermal storage LRU' claim is factually wrong: General Atomics (in-house, P3R2-D-13-S02) and Advanced Cooling Technologies (merchant, ITAR-registered, P3R2-D-13-S03) already sell DEW PCM thermal storage at the 30 kW+ tier, with a possible Honeywell/Rocky Research third incumbent unverified. Differentiation (300+ MJ MW-class vs 3 MJ demonstrated) is real but incumbents are positioned to scale. Flip: Honeywell confirmation would push this toward fail-adjacent.

G7.rationale: Dated triggers inside the window: JLWS 150 kW -> 300-500 kW production-ready systems (P3R2-D-13-S01), E-HEL FY2027 procurement (S06), HELSI megawatt-class scaling, with program consolidation (S04) concentrating rather than diluting the MW-thermal market.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: Active funded procurement: JLWS OTAs with $847M ceiling and named awardees (P3R2-D-13-S01); E-HEL 24-unit program of record (S06). Thermal-magazine demand is derived but mechanically coupled to the published power roadmap.

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: Thermal energy stored like ammunition is a compelling system concept, though the underlying PCM technology is mature.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: Narrow (MW-class DEW platforms), high-value, reachable via SBIR/primes (named OTA holders); but GA and ACT are already inside the niche (S02/S03).

competition_whitespace.evidence: GA in-house since 2010 (S02), ACT merchant and ITAR-registered (S03), possible Honeywell third; whitespace is only the unproven MW/300 MJ scale tier.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: $220k brassboard near band; v1 $3-8M with SBIR non-dilutive path makes sub-$5M sellable v1 plausible.

ideas.physics_build_review: Closed-Brayton He-Xe conversion has real flight-adjacent heritage (BRU lineage, Kilopower, Creare's current 26%-efficiency small-scale work per P3R2-D-16-S06); the physics is settled and the difficulty is flight-weight engineering -- foil bearings at ~90 krpm, specific-mass targets, radiator-matched interfaces, and kV-class lunar PMAD with Paschen-minimum and dust constraints. TRL 3 is fair. The FIX posture (studies/SBIR only, no hardware capex until the ~April 2027 downselect and contracted flight dates; 2028 checkpoint) is the correct pre-company structure and is executable at near-zero capital; the conditional $500k 10 kWe heater-loop rig is realistic only with a university/NASA-partner lab. Service burden is not applicable in the usual sense -- this is a government flight program with 1-3 units, so the revenue model is subcontracts, which the $10-30M v1 capital must be justified against. The showstopper is programmatic, not physical: one buyer, a program that already re-baselined 40->100 kWe once, and a merchant slot that Brayton Energy and Creare already partially occupy.

G1.rationale: Demand is real, official, and funded: NSTM-3 directs >=100 kWe reactors for the 2030s with downselect by ~April 2027 (P3R2-D-16-S01), and Congress appropriated $250M for FSP in FY2026 (S02) -- money moving, not just a memo. Buyers are the three named concept teams (S03).

G2.rationale: Technical claims rest on NASA program heritage sources (L09-105, L09-031/033) and fetched primary reporting; no unreviewed academic dependency.

G3.rationale: Bounded studies/SBIR de-risking exists for 2026-27, but the decisive technical experiment ($500k rig) is 2x the budget band and capital-gated behind external program events (downselect, flight-date contracts) the founder cannot influence.

G4.rationale: Competitors named, but the evidence corrects the whitespace: Brayton Energy LLC is an existing independent merchant Brayton house (S05) and Creare sits inside the Lockheed team (S06); the 'merchant multi-team supplier position is open' claim is narrower than seeded.

G5.rationale: No exotic physics or unlimited capital, but the market is one government program with 1-3 flight units through 2034 -- effectively a single program-continuation bet, honestly held as an option with a 2033-12 kill.

G6.rationale: FSP is restricted to domestic US firms (S04) -- favorable for a US entrant; standard NASA flight-qualification and nuclear-adjacent safety review acknowledged; no blocked route.

G7.rationale: Official dated triggers squarely in window (NSTM-3 Q1 FY2030 launch-readiness targeting + FY2026 appropriation), but the program has re-baselined once, the idea's own 2028 checkpoint is not yet satisfiable (downselect pending), and a slip past 2034 reduces the market to study contracts.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: NSTM-3 directive (P3R2-D-16-S01) + $250M FY2026 appropriation (S02): funded federal demand, but for reactors -- PCU subcontract demand is structural inference and the downselect has not occurred.

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: A fire-extinguisher-sized He-Xe turbine at 90,000 rpm above a nuclear core on the Moon, extending to Mars ISRU and NEP tugs (S01, L09-105) -- maximal frontier vision with a real program behind it.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: Narrow and valuable (three named teams all needing conversion, L09-031/033), reachable via SBIR; partially served by Creare/Brayton Energy.

competition_whitespace.evidence: Brayton Energy already merchant (S05), Creare embedded (S06), primes captive; the multi-team merchant wedge is plausible but contested.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: Program risk is testable cheaply via studies, but the technical key-risk test is $500k and sellable v1 requires $10-30M -- far beyond the $5M rubric line.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Modellable cycle with heritage anchors (L09-105); bounded rig-before-flight steps; but flight qualification stretches beyond bounded pre-company iteration.

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: The edge is a business-model position (one validated PCU sold to all teams), not an order-of-magnitude technical advantage over heritage designs.

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: US-only, single-program; domestically protected but narrow. No CN leg possible or claimed.

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: NSTM-3 dates (downselect ~2027, 2030s flights) align precisely with a 2030 launch, discounted for demonstrated re-baselining risk (S01/S03).

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: Founder_fit_note: PMAD/alternator control fits, but He-Xe turbomachinery, foil bearings, and flight qualification dominate and are outside the profile.

Scored as the option the seed honestly declares itself to be; if the 2028 checkpoint passes with contracted flight dates, demand and window scores would rise materially.

ideas.physics_build_review: Varactor- and liquid-crystal-tuned metasurface beam steering at millimeter wave is demonstrated in the literature (L16-010/011/013/014); the open physics questions at 30-40 GHz are insertion loss (every dB erodes the cost-per-steered-watt thesis), tuning speed against the topic's 10-20 us beam-switching spec (varactors comfortably fast; LC likely milliseconds -- the unit-cell choice is load-bearing), sidelobe control across +/-45 degrees, and transmit power handling. TRL 3 is fair. The 2026-29 path is SBIR-paced and executable by a university-affiliated team: a $250k 64-element tile experiment directly measures the governing quantities, and the architecture's cost logic (a handful of RF chains replacing thousands of T/R modules) makes the Army's $300k/unit target credible if losses stay acceptable. Capex is modest (PCB/LTCC supply chains). Service burden low (sealed tiles + software). Showstopper: aperture efficiency at 35 GHz may force premium substrates/components, eroding exactly the affordability edge that is the entire thesis, while GaN AESA costs fall in parallel.

G1.rationale: A real, funded, product-specific government solicitation exists -- Army SBIR A254-049, opened 2026-04-15, $300k/unit production target, verified on the Army topic page (P3R2-D-18-S01) -- plus category-level counter-UAS procurement proof (Echodyne's $490M USAF SUADS IDIQ, S04). But no Phase I award announced yet, no second Ka-band-specific anchor, and the seed's own 2028 second-anchor gate is unmet. Flip: no Phase I/II award or second program office by 2028 = fail.

G2.rationale: Metasurface technical basis is peer-reviewed atlas material (L16-010/011/013/014); the KRISS-to-KER item was correctly re-classified as tangential (FSS radome, not ESA) and excluded from the base case (S06).

G3.rationale: $250k 64-element tile experiment against the draft spec (2027) -- at the band edge, executable, with SBIR Phase I as non-dilutive cover.

G4.rationale: Echodyne is far larger than the seed assumed ($40M/30,000-radar-per-year facility S03; $490M IDIQ S04) though its fielded products are K-band, not Ka; GaN AESA cost curves are falling. The Ka-band affordable-tile difference is real today but must be re-verified at award time.

G5.rationale: Physics is demonstrated at lower maturity; the loss/tuning-speed intersection at 35 GHz is unproven but testable; initial market ($3-15M for 10-50 range units) is small but verifiable, not a grand forecast.

G6.rationale: Standard ITAR/USML Cat XI defense-radar controls; US-only base case; no blocked route.

G7.rationale: The only primary trigger is the 2026 SBIR topic whose 2028-2035 extension runs through contingent Phase II/III success; the second timing signal (Echodyne scaling now) is double-edged. Timing thesis is explicit and window-risk analyzed, but the two-independent-timing-sources bar is met only weakly.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: Product-specific government solicitation with explicit cost target (P3R2-D-18-S01) + funded counter-UAS complex (S04); single-threaded, no award yet.

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: Software-defined refraction from printed sub-wavelength resonators, with RIS/6G expansion (L16-010/011) -- a genuine architecture leap over T/R-module arrays.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: The Army's own solicitation states Ka-band ESA is unaffordable for test/training -- a named, reachable, underserved beachhead with a published price anchor (S01, L16-020).

competition_whitespace.evidence: Echodyne's scale, metamaterial IP, and manufacturing capacity (S03/S04) make it a fast-follower one band-extension away; GaN cost decline compresses the gap.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: $250k decisive tile test at band edge; v1 $5-12M exceeds the $5M line but SBIR Phase II/III non-dilutive funding is the designed path.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Unit cells are full-wave-simulable, tiles are bench-measurable, and the 64->256-element ladder is bounded (L16-013/014).

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: A handful of RF chains vs thousands of T/R modules targets ~10x cost per steered watt -- the Army's $1M-3M-class conventional alternative vs the $300k target frames the edge (S01, L16-020).

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: US-only (ITAR); the US case is real but thin (one topic); Guangqi (S05) is competitor context, not a market.

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: SBIR Phase III sole-source rights are a protected on-ramp timed to 2030, contingent on Phase II; GaN cost convergence is the window risk.

expansion_economics.evidence: Counter-UAS volume, range instrumentation, and comms/RIS share the tile technology.

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: Founder_fit_note: direct application of electromagnetics, RF circuits, numerical analysis, and embedded skills to unit cells, calibration, and beam control.

ideas.uncertainty_notes: Single-threaded demand is the dominant risk: the score assumes the A254-049 pipeline progresses; a Phase I non-award or a 2028 second-anchor miss drops this to kill per the seed's own gate. Physics risk (loss/tuning-speed at 35 GHz) is real but cheaply testable.

ideas.physics_build_review: Composite rotors on active magnetic bearings in vacuum are established engineering (Active Power/Piller and Stornetic's centrifuge-heritage EnWheel prove the class); millisecond response via inverter coupling is standard power electronics, and the governing unknowns -- rotor loss growth over 100k+ microcycles, AMB robustness under continuous dither -- are directly measurable on the planned $350k 50 kW/2 MJ subscale rig. TRL 4 is honest. The FIX-hardened 2026-27 instrumented load-spectrum study is the correct and cheap first step: the microcycle buffering niche must be measured against what rack capacitance and software smoothing will absorb. Build path is executable (university spin lab with burst-containment infrastructure), though containment certification for 40 krpm composite rotors and per-module cost versus supercap/BESS hybrids are the real productization hurdles; v1 at $8-20M is honest for containerized strings. Service burden is meaningful: vacuum integrity, AMB electronics fleets, rotor inspection. No physics showstopper; the showstopper is that the niche may be erased in software before 2030.

G1.rationale: A named buyer has already procured exactly this architecture class: the 400 MW East Coast off-grid AI plant (E-Finity EPC) deployed Piller SHIELDX flywheel stabilization with Bergen engines (P3R2-D-19-S01, primary release); GE Vernova's $2.4B/quarter datacenter electrification orders (S02, filing-grade) independently corroborate the buildout.

G2.rationale: Technical basis (L16-037/038 flywheel engineering) is atlas-accepted; no unreviewed academic dependency.

G3.rationale: The decisive commercial-risk test (instrumented load-spectrum study with a datacenter partner) is executable within the budget band; the $350k rotor rig follows in 2028 with quantitative pass bars.

G4.rationale: Incumbents named and fetched: Piller (incumbent on the flagship project, S01, with Austin manufacturing via Active Power S04), Stornetic (composite/AMB analog, S03), plus BESS/supercap substitutes (L08-036/037); DC-native composite-rotor microcycle economics is a non-cosmetic difference to be proven.

G5.rationale: Decades-established physics; the niche-sizing question is honestly structured as measure-not-assert; no single unverifiable forecast is load-bearing.

G6.rationale: Civilian industrial product; UL/IEEE interconnection and rotating-machinery safety paths standard; CN correctly scoped to a license note only.

G7.rationale: Dated triggers: SHIELDX procured 2025 with 2025-2027 delivery (S01), NVIDIA Kyber/800VDC full-scale production 2027 creating the DC-coupled socket (S05), GE Vernova order wave (S02); window persists into the 2030s with BESS/software-smoothing risk analyzed.

ideas.physics_build_review: Molten-tin pumping at 1,200-1,400 C is demonstrated at lab scale (MIT lineage, L04-108; Fourth Power's own record pump per P3R2-D-20-S02), and a $300k 1,000 h instrumented tin loop is a genuinely executable, novel-data-producing experiment at a university high-temperature lab. But the product spec reaches 1,900-2,400 C molten silicon/tin (L04-001/002/009), a regime where liquid silicon aggressively attacks nearly all containment chemistry and where seals, valves, and instrumentation are largely unsolved -- TRL 3 is generous above ~1,600 C. The build path's first two years are sound engineering; the fatal problem is that the entire buyer population for qualified hot hydraulics is currently one pre-commercial company (Fourth Power, $45M raised, unnamed utility letters of interest, no order book, S01/S02) while the competing solid-media architectures that need none of this plumbing are commercially ahead (Rondo's named deployments, S03; Antora's SCALEUP lineage). Capex and service models cannot be constructed against a market of single-digit demonstration skids.

G1.rationale: No primary buyer/procurement source exists for liquid-metal transport hardware: Fourth Power has no signed commercial order (unnamed utility interest only, P3R2-D-20-S01/S02), a targeted search for any alternative named buyer found nothing, and the seed's own 2028 order-book gate is unsatisfied. Demand is not proven -- stated plainly per the evidence file.

G2.rationale: Technical claims (TPV efficiency at 1,900-2,400 C, molten-silicon pumping) rest on peer-reviewed and institutional sources (L04-001/002/009/108; MIT News S02).

G3.rationale: $300k 1,000 h molten-tin loop with measurable seal-wear/flow/attack outputs is bounded and would generate genuinely scarce lifetime data.

G4.rationale: Competitor/substitute map is honest and confirmed: in-house builds at Antora/Fourth Power, no merchant supplier (absence corroborated), and the solid-media substitution named as the architectural risk (S03).

G5.rationale: The demand case depends on a single unverifiable market contingency -- liquid-metal thermal batteries beating solid media -- and the refreshed evidence shows the opposite trajectory (Rondo shipping to named customers S03; Antora ahead; Fourth Power pre-commercial), plus unproven materials physics in the 1,900-2,400 C silicon regime.

G6.rationale: No export-control or certification blocker; standard high-temperature process-safety codes.

G7.rationale: No primary/official 2028-2035 trigger exists -- the only dated trigger is Fourth Power's own company roadmap ('GWh by 2029'), and the independent comparator signal (Rondo) times the competing architecture, not this one. The window may never open, and cannot be shown open by 2030 on current evidence.

ideas.elimination_reason: G1 fail (plus G5 and G7 fails): demand for qualified liquid-metal hot hydraulics is entirely hostage to a single pre-commercial company with no order book, no alternative buyer exists, no official timing trigger exists, and the evidenced market trajectory favors solid-media architectures that eliminate the product category. The seed's own 2028 kill gate is well-designed; as of 2026-07 its condition is failed, and P4 promotion would require fabricating a demand case the evidence explicitly negates.
ideas.uncertainty_notes: Elimination robust. Revival conditions: a liquid-metal thermal-battery developer signs a named commercial order (the seed's own 2028 gate), or an independent buyer class (solar-thermochemical, metallurgy) issues a dated procurement for >1,400 C liquid-metal transport. The tin-loop experiment would still be scientifically valuable but is not a venture predicate.

Re-test Fourth Power's order book at the 2028 checkpoint before any reconsideration.

ideas.physics_build_review: Capacitive+ultrasonic vapor-quality sensing, dryout-margin observers, and MPC of pump/expansion are established two-phase instrumentation and controls techniques; the <5% quality-estimation error target against gamma-densitometry reference is ambitious but bench-testable, and the fluid-property auto-identification layer is the genuinely novel and hardest-to-certify element. The $250k two-channel rig (2027, three low-GWP fluids, 10x heat-flux steps) is executable in a university thermal lab and directly measures the product's core claims; v1 capital of $2-6M is the most realistic in this batch because the product is an electronics module plus algorithms. Service burden is low. There is no physics showstopper -- the showstoppers are structural: (a) OEMs may simply build the control layer in-house once one fluid wins (company-vs-feature), and (b) the whole premium depends on two-phase actually displacing single-phase at scale, which Dell'Oro projects only 'gradually'; the seed's own 2028 two-OEM-agreement gate and <5%-share-by-2033 kill trigger are the correct instruments for both.

ideas.physics_build_review: Superconducting/low-conductance flex laminates and 4K cryo-CMOS multiplexers are individually demonstrated in the literature (L13-004/005/022/023, L07-014..017); the product's contribution is co-engineering them against the fridge's stage-by-stage heat budget, which is genuinely hard systems work with unforgiving failure modes (crosstalk at mK, mux dissipation against milliwatt budgets, readout-fidelity parity). TRL 3 is honest. The 2026-29 path (flex trade studies, MPW mux tapeout, 256-channel segment, DOE testbed pilot) is executable by a university cryolab team, but the $500k first-experiment budget likely understates cumulative MPW + fridge-time costs, and cryo iteration cycles are slow. A material new constraint: the September 2024 BIS interim rule (ECCN 3A904 and related cryo/quantum ECCNs, P3R2-E-04-S01) imposes licensing even for allies, so the SEEQC-ITRI Taiwan manufacturing wedge and JP/KR testbeds carry unpriced licensing lead time, plus deemed-export exposure in the academic phase. Service burden moderate (qualified subsystem with integration support). Showstopper watch: architectural substitution -- IBM's own scaling path is modular chip-to-chip links (Cockatoo 2027) rather than more external channels, and on-chip 1000:1 mux or optical links could deflate the harness-density wall the product monetizes.

ideas.physics_build_review: The component physics is sound and unspectacular: lid-integrated passive two-phase spreaders, micro-TEC islands holding +/-0.5 C at laser sites, and validated TIM stacks are all established arts, and the 2027 testbed (ASIC + 8 laser sites, 30% power transient, <3% cooling overhead) is a well-designed $300k experiment. TRL 3 is fair. The problem is not buildability but the socket: the refreshed evidence shows the package-thermal white space being filled by the supply chain itself before a pre-company team could even reach its 2028 gate -- ASE is ramping its in-house FOEB thermal-warpage solution into volume production in H2 2026 for 1.6T FOCoS-CPO packages and TSMC's COUPE integrates the optical engine on-substrate in 2026 (P3R2-E-11-S05), while Broadcom and Marvell are architecturally relocating lasers to the faceplate to avoid in-package stabilization entirely (S01, S04). A merchant BOM-item vendor entering in 2030 would face OSATs that already own qualified in-house solutions and switch vendors that designed the problem away; the honest bottom-up sizing lands at or below $6M of 2030 revenue. Service burden (package-level qualification per customer) is heavy for a component vendor.

G1.rationale: Category demand for CPO is real and dated (Broadcom Bailly deployed at named hyperscalers, P3R2-E-11-S01; OCP consortium S03), but there is no procurement signal for a merchant package-thermal module, and Coherent's $4B TAM (L12-035/036) is vendor framing from a party that is simultaneously buyer and competitor.

G2.rationale: The four-architecture non-convergence claim rests on peer-reviewed 2024-26 treatments (L12-021..024, L14-014..018).

G3.rationale: $300k 2027 testbed with quantitative photonics-grade pass bars is bounded and executable in a university packaging lab.

G4.rationale: Competitors named and now sharpened by evidence: ASE FOEB, TSMC COUPE, Broadcom/Marvell in-house and architectural, Mikros (S01/S04/S05); the +/-0.5 C laser-site spec is a non-cosmetic differentiator on paper.

G5.rationale: No exotic physics; all components are established; sizing assumptions labeled.

G6.rationale: No product-specific export control; TW supply-chain leg unrestricted for this product class.

G7.rationale: The merchant window is being pre-empted years before a 2030 launch: ASE in-house thermal solution in volume production H2 2026, TSMC substrate integration 2026, and the two most-named buyers (Broadcom, Marvell) pursuing laser-relocation architectures instead of buying stabilization modules (S01, S05). The seed's binding 2028 gate requires a switch-vendor/OSAT co-development agreement whose plausible counterparties are all solving the problem in-house -- the opportunity window is closing before 2030, failing the launch-window requirement.

ideas.elimination_reason: G7 fail: the 2030 launch window is already closing -- OSAT/foundry in-house thermal solutions (ASE FOEB volume production H2 2026; TSMC COUPE 2026) and buyer-side architectural workarounds (Broadcom and Marvell relocating lasers to the faceplate) are absorbing the package-thermal socket four years before company launch, exactly the 'absorbed entirely by OSAT roadmaps' risk the seed itself flagged, now evidenced as underway rather than hypothetical. Product-specific demand was never evidenced (G1 marginal), and the realistic merchant residual sits at or below $6M/yr by 2030.
ideas.uncertainty_notes: Elimination rests on supply-chain trajectory rather than a hard legal/physical wall, so it carries somewhat more reversal risk than the batch's other eliminations. Revival conditions: a switch vendor or OSAT signs a thermal co-development agreement with an outside party, or ring-based CPO generations demonstrably fail on wavelength stability at volume, reopening a stabilization-module socket ASE/TSMC solutions cannot serve.

Test whether LPO/ring-less generations defer the CPO thermal problem entirely, which would kill even the residual thesis.

No physics showstopper; the structural risk is commercial gatekeeping -- converter vendors (Hitachi/Siemens/GE) bundle protection with their stations and may refuse third-party relays, which is why the open-benchmark/qualification-authority strategy (selling the test everyone must pass) is the load-bearing design choice.

ideas.physics_build_review: Nothing here strains physics: ppm-class DC converters, energy-extraction/dump circuits, HTS/binary current leads, and busbars are mature arts individually -- the product is integration, standardization, and a certified acceptance protocol. TRL 6 is defensible for the components but generous for the integrated skid, which does not yet exist as a product. The 2026-29 path (acceptance-protocol drafting, $400k 25 kA-class converter+dump demo on a university HTS coil, entry-SKU 5 kA instrumented leads with certified heat-load datasheets and first lab POs by 2029) is executable and the entry SKU is a genuinely realistic early-revenue wedge. The fetched incumbency map cuts hard, though: OCEM already delivers both precision converters (50 kA, 5 ppm) and fusion switching-network/dump units (JT-60SA, 20 kA in <1 ms; DTT safety units), and Danfysik advertises 0.1 ppm stability -- tighter than F-02's own 10 ppm target (P3R2-F-02-S01/S02). The white space is therefore only the standardized catalog + leads + protocol bundle, which 30-year incumbents could assemble quickly if a buyer asked. Service burden is high (site commissioning of kA-class hardware). Showstopper watch: CFS vertical integration (not yet realized per S06) and fusion schedule slip, hedged by accelerator/research-magnet demand.

G1.rationale: US leg dual-sourced and real: CFS-Realta long-term magnet partnership with multi-billion potential (P3R2-F-02-S03) and the DOE Milestone program ($415M through FY2027, 8 named companies, S04). But the skid itself has no named buyer, and the CN leg's named buyer claim (ASIPP PF/RMP tenders) could not be independently confirmed (S07 shows magnet-building scale, not power-supply procurement). Flip: no drive-and-dump LOI from a merchant-magnet buyer by 2028.

G2.rationale: Technical basis (L03-018/020/021, L05-003, L07-009) is atlas-accepted engineering literature.

G3.rationale: The $400k converter+dump demo is 1.6x the band and needs a university HTS coil partnership; the entry-SKU lead prototype track is cheaper and carries the real early de-risking.

G4.rationale: The binding P4 incumbency-map condition is delivered: OCEM (S01) and Danfysik (S02) confirmed as live, decades-deep incumbents whose catalogs cover the converter and (for OCEM) dump-unit components; the surviving whitespace -- integrated island + acceptance protocol + catalog leads -- is real but thin and quickly replicable.

G5.rationale: Mature physics; dual demand legs (fusion program + merchant magnet commerce + accelerator hedge) avoid single-forecast dependence.

G6.rationale: ASIPP returns no sanctions/Entity-List match (S05, aggregator-level negative); big-science procurement routes with a 2027 export-counsel gate; dual-use EAR review acknowledged as process risk, not a block.

G7.rationale: Two independent dated triggers, one official: DOE Milestone program milestones toward mid-2030s FPPs (S04) and CFS/Realta's commercial-plant targets (S03); hedge demand (research/industrial magnets, L03-035) exists now if fusion slips.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: Real, dual-sourced US magnet-market demand (P3R2-F-02-S03/S04) one derivation step from the product; no skid buyer yet; CN buyer claim unverified (S07).

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: 'Power island in a box' for the superconducting fleet is a solid platform vision built on integration rather than a technical leap.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: Merchant magnet buyers (Realta/WHAM/Type One per S03/S06) are named and reachable; the niche is real but shared with incumbents one bundling decision away.

competition_whitespace.evidence: OCEM already sells converters and fusion dump/safety units (S01); Danfysik beats the stability spec (S02); CFS could bundle (S06). Whitespace survives 'only barely' per the delivered map.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: Entry-SKU (5 kA leads, certified datasheets) is a sub-$250k-class de-risk path with 2029 revenue potential; the full skid demo is $400k and v1 $5-12M.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Everything is bench-instrumentable (stability, extraction waveforms, heat load per lead) with mature measurement standards (L03-018/020/021).

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: No order-of-magnitude claim: 10 ppm trails Danfysik's 0.1 ppm; the edge is catalog standardization and certified pairing with quench detection.

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: Solid US leg (S03/S04); CN leg plausible (ASIPP's 100%-domestic magnet buildout, S07) but unverified as procurement -- scored as US-solid plus CN-optionality, not dual-strong.

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: DOE milestones and CFS/Realta commercial targets date serial magnet builds to post-2030 (S03/S04), with the mid-2030s tail leaving slip exposure; hedge demand exists now.

expansion_economics.evidence: Accelerators, industrial field coils, and the entry-SKU catalog extend the platform beyond fusion.

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: Founder_fit_note: converter control, dump sequencing, acceptance protocols fit; kA-class power hardware and busbar/thermal engineering outside.

ideas.uncertainty_notes: The demand derivation (magnets are selling, therefore drive-and-dump will sell to the same buyers) is plausible but unclosed; the incumbency map shows the thinnest whitespace among the batch's advancing dual-market ideas. CN leg should not be scored equal to US until an ASIPP-class tender is verified.

ideas.physics_build_review: A 250 kW/30 krpm PM generator with an active front-end converter is established engineering -- Calnetix's Carefree IPM ships essentially this concept (generator + magnetic bearings + power electronics in one hermetic unit) at the 125 kW class today (P3R2-F-03-S06). Scaling the cartridge concept across 0.3-20 MW classes is a serious rotordynamics/thermal/converter program well beyond the seed's $5M-adjacent early capital, and sCO2 duty adds Sandia-documented unresolved rotor/bearing integration challenges (L04-113): high fluid density, windage, hot restarts. TRL 5 is fair at the small end only. The plan's staging is honest -- the $900k drive-stand build (3.6x the budget band) is explicitly gated behind 2026-27 requirements interviews with 5+ machine builders, which cost almost nothing and test the real risk: whether any turbomachinery OEM will externalize its electrical island at all, given Turboden and Ormat are vertically integrated (S01/S07) and Calnetix already serves the merchant slot at small scale. Service burden is high (rotating machinery in industrial plants, grid-code compliance per market). Showstopper: the merchant socket may simply not exist at venture scale.

G1.rationale: Underlying demand is real and dual-sourced on both legs: Fervo-Turboden 1.75 GW/35-GeoBlock framework with buyer-stated supply-bottleneck rationale (P3R2-F-03-S01/S03) and Chaotan One in commercial operation (S04/S05). But no machine builder has bought or committed to a merchant cartridge, and the counterparties in every cited transaction are vertically integrated. Flip: the binding 2029 co-development gate.

G2.rationale: Technical basis (L04-102/106/111/113/109) is atlas-accepted; Sandia's unresolved-challenges framing is honestly cited against the idea.

G3.rationale: The decisive early test (requirements interviews) is nearly free, but the first hardware experiment is $900k -- 3.6x the band -- and correctly staged behind the interview findings.

G4.rationale: The binding P4 incumbent-map condition is delivered and it bites: Calnetix ships the exact concept at 125 kW (S06), Turboden/Ormat are vertically integrated (S01/S07), Hanwha is an active competitor not just a buyer (S08). Differentiation narrows to scale (0.3-20 MW) and sCO2 duty class -- real but unproven.

G5.rationale: Established physics; dual-leg demand; the single-source CN retrofit TAM (L04-051) is explicitly quarantined behind a pre-scale triangulation gate rather than assumed.

G6.rationale: No Entity List exposure on named counterparties; ordinary EAR review for CNNC-affiliated projects acknowledged; grid-code compliance is the certification path.

G7.rationale: Dated triggers on both legs (Fervo framework deliveries from 2026, S01/S03; Chaotan One operating 2025-12 with the ~2028 Xinjiang first-set as the seed's own atlas-dated follow-on, S04), but the window is conditional on the open 2029 co-development gate and un-triangulated CN scale -- opens conditionally, per the evidence file's own verdict.

demonstrated_demand.evidence: Dual-sourced turbine-level demand both legs (P3R2-F-03-S01/S03/S04/S05) with buyer-stated supply constraints; zero cartridge-level buyers.

frontier_coolness_vision.evidence: Commoditizing the electrical half of small turbomachinery is useful industrial infrastructure, not a frontier leap.

high_end_niche_quality.evidence: The machine-builder OEM niche is occupied at small scale by Calnetix (S06) and closed off by vertical integration at the named buyers (S01/S07).

competition_whitespace.evidence: Calnetix ships the concept; Turboden/Ormat/Hanwha integrate; CN institute ecosystem in-house (L04-102/106). Whitespace is a scale/duty band, not a category.

reachable_validation_budget.evidence: Interviews are cheap and decisive, but the hardware key-risk test is $900k and v1 is $8-20M -- far past the $5M line.

technical_elegance_controllability.evidence: Drive-stand emulation of load transients, grid-code ride-through, and hot-restart tests are all bounded, instrumentable engineering (L04-113 defines the checklist).

tenx_technical_edge.evidence: No order-of-magnitude edge; the offer is standardization and certified rotordynamic co-optimization.

us_china_dual_market_leverage.evidence: Genuine dated evidence on both legs (Fervo US; Chaotan One CN, S04/S05) -- the CN leg is real but single-project and untriangulated.

launch_window_fit_2030.evidence: Fervo buildout and CN first-set follow-on logic both point into 2030-2034 (S01, S04), conditional on the 2029 gate.

expansion_economics.evidence: sCO2, ORC, air-cycle, and CAES machines share the cartridge family if the socket exists.

founder_skill_transfer.evidence: Founder_fit_note: AFE control, bearing-drive electronics, grid-code testing fit; PM machine design and turbomachinery integration outside.

ideas.uncertainty_notes: Weakest advancing idea in the batch: gates pass on staging discipline and dual-leg underlying demand, but the merchant-socket hypothesis is untested and the closest incumbent already owns the concept. The near-free interview program should be treated as the real gate; a negative finding kills before any capital is at risk.
redteam_flags_for_p5: Attack the externalization premise directly: will Turboden, Ormat, or any sCO2 builder name a price at which they would buy a third-party electrical island?
Assess Calnetix's MW-class scaling intent -- they own the concept and the references.

ideas.physics_build_review: Five-axis AMB control, adaptive unbalance rejection, and touchdown-bearing life management are mature engineering with well-documented open problems (L07-010..013); a self-commissioning controller is a real but incremental contribution, and the $350k turbopump-retrofit experiment with shock-ride-through and teardown-validated life estimation is bounded, well-designed, and squarely in the founder's skill set. TRL 5 is fair. None of that rescues the commercial structure: the thesis lives or dies on pump/machine OEMs externalizing AMB electronics, and this session found no evidence any OEM does or will -- Ebara ships integrated onboard controllers (P3R2-F-04-S02), SKF S2M's decades of semiconductor-lineage merchant supply show the category is not greenfield (S01), and Synchrony adds a fourth incumbent. On the CN side the picture is affirmatively hostile: domestic maglev-turbopump vendors (Kuntai at 70-80% of import pricing with orders booked, S06) are substituting fast, and NAURA -- one of the two named buyer-ecosystem anchors -- is BIS Entity-Listed (S03/S05). Capex and service models are unremarkable; the missing ingredient is any evidenced buyer.

G1.rationale: No buyer-level demand evidence exists on either leg: no named paid OEM evaluation in the US (the externalization thesis is explicitly untested), and the CN leg fails the seed's own binding fix condition -- no named CN pump/tool-OEM design-in channel was found, only fast domestic substitution (P3R2-F-04-S06). The documented AMB reliability literature (L07-010..013) is technical-possibility evidence, not demand, per mission rule 5.

G2.rationale: Technical claims rest on atlas-accepted engineering literature (L07-010..013, L04-113).

G3.rationale: $350k 2027 retrofit experiment with quantitative shock/teardown endpoints is bounded and executable.

G4.rationale: The required teardown was delivered and narrows the wedge: SKF S2M has deep semiconductor/turbopump lineage (S01), Synchrony is a fourth incumbent, and Ebara's integrated controllers leave the externalization question unresolved (S02); the semiconductor-grade-duty gap is partially supported but not clear.

G5.rationale: Mature physics; assumptions labeled; no single grand forecast -- the problem is absence of demand, not reliance on a bad forecast.

G6.rationale: US leg clean, but a named CN buyer-ecosystem anchor (NAURA) is Entity-Listed with restrictive licensing (S03/S05), and the seed's own condition forces the primary-market downgrade to US-only; the CN route is effectively closed rather than merely conditioned.

G7.rationale: No primary/official 2028-2035 trigger exists for the US externalization thesis (the evidence file states the window 'is not independently datable'), and the CN window is closing now under accelerating domestic substitution (S05/S06) -- the required two-independent-timing-sources bar, including one official trigger, is not met.

ideas.elimination_reason: G1 and G7 fail: no buyer-level demand evidence exists for merchant AMB electronics on either leg; the seed's own binding CN condition (named design-in channel or downgrade) is failed with the CN niche instead showing fast domestic substitution and an Entity-Listed anchor buyer; and no datable 2028-2035 demand trigger supports the 2030 launch thesis. The idea reduces to an untested externalization hypothesis against four established merchant incumbents.
ideas.uncertainty_notes: Elimination robust on the demand and timing gates. Revival condition: a named pump/compressor OEM signs the 2028 paid evaluation the seed itself specifies -- that single event would reopen G1, though the incumbent teardown would still cap competition scores.

Verify Zhongke Instruments and Feixuan Technology claims to primary-source standard to complete the CN substitution picture.

ideas.physics_build_review: There is no physics risk: 650-1000 V pack conversion, harsh-duty traction inverters, VCUs, and dual-personality charge interfaces are commodity engineering, and TRL 6 is fair; the IEC TS 63379 MCS spec is now published (2026-02-09, P3R2-F-05-S06), making the interface plan current. The $600k grant-funded pilot repower is executable. The build problem is that heterogeneous legacy fleets resist kit standardization -- each vehicle class is per-project integration engineering, which is precisely why incumbents (Danfoss Editron, positioned as the number-one harbor/port electrification system supplier, S04; Dana TM4, S05) sell OEM system projects rather than kits. The certification-matrix moat (UL/IEC/GB certified once, reused) is a real idea neither incumbent visibly offers, but every demand mechanism it attaches to failed refresh: EPA Clean Ports funds new-equipment purchases with no repower category (S01/S02), the CARB drayage mandate is in active repeal with final action due 2026-08-31 (S07), the unsubsidized repower economics model does not yet exist, and the flagship CN OEM export deal (XCMG-Fortescue, S03) completed without any third-party certification intermediary. A 2030 launch would enter after the grant wave ends with no evidenced second wave.

G1.rationale: Neither leg has a primary demand source for the actual products: EPA's $3B funds new zero-emission equipment, not repower kits (confirmed against the idea's own honesty flag, P3R2-F-05-S01/S02); no repower-specific award exists; the CARB tailwind is in active repeal (S07); and the CN certification-service model has no confirmed buyer -- the XCMG-Fortescue deal (S03) demonstrates the trend proceeding without the proposed service. Adjacent-category spending cannot substitute for product-level demand under mission rule 5.

G2.rationale: No load-bearing academic claims; sources are government, company, and standards documents.

G3.rationale: The $600k 2028 pilot is bounded but 2.4x the band and dependent on riding a port's grant award; the truly decisive cheap test (unsubsidized repower economics model) is correctly scheduled but not yet done.

G4.rationale: The required teardown was delivered: Danfoss Editron is already positioned in harbor equipment (S04) and Dana TM4 in off-highway systems (S05), confirming the seed's own downgrade of the 'no standardized kit' claim; the tri-standard certification matrix remains unaddressed by incumbents but is unproven as a purchasable product.

G5.rationale: No physics or capital fantasy, but the US leg depends on an unevidenced behavioral forecast (owners repowering unsubsidized in a 2030-2034 second wave) and the CN leg on an unevidenced service-demand hypothesis -- two unverified market layers stacked on real adjacent trends.

G6.rationale: Certification pathways (UL/CE/CB Scheme) are established; XCMG is not Entity-Listed (S09); CATL's 1260H listing (S08) is a supply-chain diligence flag, not a bar, for this civilian context.

G7.rationale: The 2030-2034 US second wave has no independent dated trigger (the evidence file: 'the second wave itself has no independent dated trigger confirmed'), the primary regulatory driver is being repealed on a dated schedule (S07), and the CN 2028-2030 window is for equipment sales, not the certification service -- the named-trigger requirement fails on both legs.

ideas.elimination_reason: G1 and G7 fail: no primary demand source exists for repower kits or export-certification services -- EPA money funds new equipment, the CARB drayage mandate is in active repeal, the repower second wave is an unevidenced assumption, and the flagship CN export deal proceeded without any third-party certifier; meanwhile Danfoss Editron already occupies the harbor-equipment electrification niche. Real adjacent capital flows do not convert into demand for this product on any dated trigger inside 2030-2034.
ideas.uncertainty_notes: Elimination is evidence-based but the underlying capital flows are large, so reversal is conceivable: a named repower-funded award, a surviving CARB mandate after 2026-08-31, or a paid XCMG-class certification engagement would each reopen the case. None exists as of 2026-07-13.

Verify the CARB OAL final action (due 2026-08-31) -- the single dated event that most changes US demand quality.
